channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tick season is getting worse. Can managing deer help?
item.description: Health officials and researchers hope that efforts to control deer populations, which serve as "party buses" for mating ticks, can reverse the tide of ticks and the illnesses they cause.
item.content:encoded:
Health officials and researchers hope that efforts to control deer populations, which serve as "party buses" for mating ticks, can reverse the tide of ticks and the illnesses they cause.

(Image credit: Mary Swift/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: High-stakes talks between the U.S. and Iran put on hold
item.description: A planned U.S.-Iran meeting was abruptly postponed, leaving the initial agreement to end the war on shaky ground.
item.content:encoded: A planned U.S.-Iran meeting was abruptly postponed, leaving the initial agreement to end the war on shaky ground.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Who is Mohammad Bagher-Ghalibaf? The man leading Iran's negotiating team
item.description: A look at the hardliner leading Iran's negotiating team wit the U.S. to end the war.
item.content:encoded: A look at the hardliner leading Iran's negotiating team wit the U.S. to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Who won and who lost? Assessing the fallout of the Iran war
item.description: Who are the winners and losers of the war with Iran? NPR's Leila Fadel talks with Richard Haass, President Emeritus of the Council on Foreign Relations for his assessment.
item.content:encoded: Who are the winners and losers of the war with Iran? NPR's Leila Fadel talks with Richard Haass, President Emeritus of the Council on Foreign Relations for his assessment.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: These Wisconsin swing voters say Trump's war in Iran wasn't worth it
item.description: The war in Iran was a costly blunder, according to Wisconsin swing voters who participated in two online focus groups that NPR observed.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran was a costly blunder, according to Wisconsin swing voters who participated in two online focus groups that NPR observed.

(Image credit: Amirhossein Khorgooei)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: Can you taste history? We try George Washington's original beer
item.description: Hops, yeast...and a lot of molasses
item.content:encoded:
Hops, yeast...and a lot of molasses

(Image credit: Jonathan Blanc/NYPL)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: A planned Switzerland meeting between the U.S. and Iran has been put on hold, JD Vance has become the face to U.S. negotiations with Iran, Obama Presidential Center gets star-studded opening ceremony.
item.content:encoded: A planned Switzerland meeting between the U.S. and Iran has been put on hold, JD Vance has become the face to U.S. negotiations with Iran, Obama Presidential Center gets star-studded opening ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: U.S. striker Folarin Balogun could be the key in match against Australia
item.description: When the United States plays Australia in the World Cup on Friday, U.S. striker Folarin Balogun will be a key to the attack. He scored two goals in the opener against Paraguay.
item.content:encoded: When the United States plays Australia in the World Cup on Friday, U.S. striker Folarin Balogun will be a key to the attack. He scored two goals in the opener against Paraguay.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Thousands of fans celebrate the Knicks win at victory parade
item.description: New York celebrated its NBA champion Knicks Thursday with a blue and orange confetti parade attended by tens of thousands of fans.
item.content:encoded: New York celebrated its NBA champion Knicks Thursday with a blue and orange confetti parade attended by tens of thousands of fans.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Obama shapes his legacy through presidential center built on hope and change
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center allows former President Barack Obama to shape how he hopes to be remembered.
item.content:encoded: The Obama Presidential Center allows former President Barack Obama to shape how he hopes to be remembered.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea
